title: Top medalists at Rio 2016
description: Top medalists from Rio 2016 based on the number of gold medals. The snippet uses the pivot type provier to aggregate the data and shows a simple bar chart with the results.

title: Top medalists from London 2012
description: Top medalists from London 2012 games based on the number of gold medals. The snippet uses the pivot type provider to aggregate the data and shows a simple bar chart with the results.

title: Number of distinct countries in Olympics
description: Number of distinct countries competing in the Olympic games. The visualization shows that the number of distinct teams has been steadily growing since the beginning of the new Olympic games.

title: Olympic medals table by athlete
description: Table showing top 10 athletes by the number of medals together with their team and discipline in which they compete. The sample creates a simple table and uses the pivot type provider for grouping.

title: You Draw It: Carbon emissions of USA
description: Can you guess how CO2 emissions changed between 2005 and 2010 if you only see data up to 2005? Inspired by the NYT "You Draw It" series, this demo lets you draw your prediction directly on a line chart.

title: You Draw It: Olympic medals won by China
description: After going down from 76 to 53, the number of Olympic medals won by china started rising in 1988. Can you guess the rest of the trend, including the Olympic games in Beijing? (Inspired by NYT's piece mapping Obama's legacy and using the D3 code shared by authors).

title: You Guess: Population of top 6 countries of the world
description: Inspired by the "You Draw It" visualisation from the New York Times, this demo lets you guess values in a bar chart. Can you correctly rank the 6 most populous countries in the world?

title: Top countries at Rio 2016
description: Top countries from Rio 2016 based on the number of gold medals. The snippet uses the pivot type provider to aggregate the data and shows a simple bar chart with the results.

title: Guess the number of Olympic teams
description: This interactive visualization lets you guess the number of teams competing in the last 5 Olympic games. The initial values show the average number, so expect this to go down for some and up for others!

title: Guess the number of Turing people
description: The Alan Turing Institute hosts faculty fellows, research fellows, and PhD students from partner universities at its London offices. Can you guess how many people work in each category?

chains.hint: Use data source from April 2017 named 'People at the Turing in 2016/2017'

title: Guess the number of Turing people (2.)
description: The Alan Turing Institute hosts a number of faculty fellows visiting from partner universities, research fellows and PhD students working at the London offices and a also welcomes visiting researchers. Guess how many of each are there! This version uses data from the turing.ac.uk website.

chains.hint: Use data source from May 2017 named 'The Alan Turing Institute People (May 2017)'

title: Guess the number of Turing people (3.)
description: The Alan Turing Institute hosts a number of faculty fellows visiting from partner universities, research fellows and PhD students working at the London offices and a also welcomes visiting researchers. Guess how many of each are there! This version uses data from the turing.ac.uk website. (Corrected!)

title: Cambridge Faculty Fellows
description: A table showing Cambridge Faculty Fellows at the Alan Turing Institute, using data scraped from the turing.ac.uk website.

steps: The Alan Turing Institute People (7 May 2017)

title: New Rio 2016 Golds
description: Testing a new version of just the Rio data, which gives the right number of gold medals

chains.hint: Use data source from May 2017 named 'Rio 2016 Olympics'

title: Top 10 Government Departments by 2015-16 Expenditure
description: A demo of the UK Public Expenditure Statistical Analyses data showing the top 10 government departments by spending in 2015-16, with a breakdown of who is spending your money on what.

steps: Central government own expenditure in budgets by departmental group (PESA 2016)
chains.hint: Use data source from May 2017 named 'Central government own expenditure in budgets by departmental group (PESA 2016)'

title: Most viewed Turing videos on YouTube
description: Top 10 videos from YouTube based on the number of views. This is based on the number of people who watched at least a bit of the video.

chains.hint: Use data source from May 2017 named 'YouTube views by video'

title: UK spending on tertiary education
description: A minimal demo using the UK expenditure data service

title: UK spending on tertiary education
description: A minimal demo using the UK expenditure data service. Less misleading version with Y axis starting from zero.

title: You Draw It: Carbon emissions of USA (new)
description: Can you guess how the CO2 emissions have been changing between 2005 and 2010 if you see data between 2000 and 2005? Inspired by the fantastic "You Draw It: What Got Better or Worse During Obama?s Presidency" piece in NYT (see link). New version using The Gama composable visualizations library.

title: GDP and Mark Spitz' medals
description: This is a demo showing how to add markers to a youguess line chart. It shows US GDP per capita with the number of gold medals won by Mark Spitz. As you can see, there is a very strong connection between the two... not.

chains.chainHint: This chain gets US GDP per capita from the World Bank
This chain gets Mark Spitz's Olympic gold medals by year

title: Video views per date
description: YouTube video views per date from the Alan Turing Institute YouTube channel.

chains.hint: Use data source from May 2017 named 'YouTube Views per Date'

title: Classification of the Functions of Government (COFOG)
description: COFOG classifies government expenditure data by the purpose for which funds are used. It is developed by the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).

chains.hint: Use data source from May 2017 named 'COFOG Structure'

title: Compost: CO2 emissions line chart
description: Simple visalization showing the CO2 emissions of China and USA using a new experimental compost library.

chains.chainHint: This chain gets CO2 emissions data for the United States
This chain gets CO2 emissions data for China

title: Compost: Top polluters bar chart
description: Simple visualization showing top 8 polluters based on CO2 emissions. This illustrates new experimental charting library compost.

title: You Draw It: United Kingdom CO2 emissions with axis labels
description: This example shows how to add axis labels to youdraw.line chart. Some of the other options are not yet supported, but labels work as expected!
